sentences.text: Not too long.
They took me out.
They took out, they closed it off.
They closed this whole place off.
Because I think was a bomb in it.
An American or Russian bomb was down there.
In [INAUDIBLE].
Yeah.
No, no.
We were not there.
This was-- I think was a-- a-- a-- a attack.
I think it's later.
Because when I left was halfway work, was not work enough.
It was Lager--
Kaufering.
Lager-- Lager I and Lager IV, this what I was.
These are numbers.
Lager I and Lager IV.
I think they're not-- they're not far.
I think by walking.
Well, Lager I and Lager IV, they are just-- one-- one Lager was I was workin' they build a new, I believe, a new-- a new building.
It was not a build, what I-- mentioned, a building for airplanes, for manufacturing.
And there-- there was a-- was something like this, a hole down there, I don't know-- 20 stories deep in the ground.
Below ground.
The-- above the ground was just like a round-- a round-- quite high, too.
No, no, nothing to work yet.
What they-- they build it.
They build it.
I think that this was a company, [?
Mool, ?] that they're producing airplanes.
M-O-O-L.
This what I think, [?
Mool, ?] [?
Mool. ?] I don't know if I pronounce it right or whatever it is.
And they build this and they-- they used to have-- the roof was not finished.
This whole-- whole gigantic.
This was, you know, by foot.
This was maybe 8 feet thick.
I-- over there was hundreds of Jewish people working, was just iron.
You know, they put in concrete iron.
And they tied it down, and they put in this thick iron, you know, a lot.
You know?
The whole-- this was long that the airplane can go in and-- and this--
--when it's finished.
So we used to stay and from the bottom up, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] train with-- with the cement, with everything the Germans had technique with cement.
And they used to go in 8 inches pipes, the-- the concrete up by force.
And the Jewish people were standing with the sticks.
I was standing with long sticks that fills up no empty-- no holes in the concrete.
And you know, people used to slide and fall in.
They used to cover them up [? right there. ?] Was nobody in [? trap. ?]
This was going like water, the concrete.
Yeah.
Over there I was working with-- with the machine, here I was working with the-- with the-- with the--
Pushing the concrete in.
This was till the [INAUDIBLE].
Till the end.
Till the end.
No, no.
Nothing there yet.
This was the plane.
Because you-- we saw the Americans, we saw the Russians.
You know, because--
I saw the planes.
Because when-- at-- every time was an alarm they used to chase out from this place.
Because down there, the Germans used to run in--
--to hide from the bombs.
And-- and us they used to send out on the field.
So we used to sit on the field.
And-- and we used to see the-- the things that they throw down, give up and all kinds of things.
Yeah.
Give up, you're going to [? be ?] [? destroyed. ?]
Same thing, on [INAUDIBLE].
On-- on this.
Food?
You-- you went out to work, you came back, outside was a little soup.
And sometime they give you a piece of bread.
I don't remember, no.
No.
No, this-- this-- this kapo was-- this-- no, another one.
But I-- I didn't finish this, you know.
I saved this guy from the truck.
He didn't go to--
I did not.
Take a look, my skin is wrinkled.
You see, it's wrinkled.
I go back.
Take a look.
Yes.
No, no, I didn't leave him.
I didn't leave him.
As the truck-- you know, they stood near the-- the-- the-- the-- the-- where we slept and everything.
I-- I went up from the line between the five people--
--and reached the truck.
And I-- and I-- and I-- start telling him, Moishe, Moishe, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] [? to the ?] end, you know, by the truck.
And I climbed a little bit in, and I throw him down on the floor and schlepped him into barrack and put him where the [?
Blockalteste ?] is laying-- [LAUGHS].
And covered him up.
And nobody saw it.
A miracle.
He survived.
He is in Israel.
I was crazy anyway.
I-- I wasn't afraid for anything.
No, this-- this-- this was Kaufering, yeah.
Nothing.
We were sitting on the edge, sitting on the edge.
Take off this [NON-ENGLISH], this one thing and take the lice out, squeeze it.
They would dig like a-- a quarter inch.
I don't know.
I don't know.
I have no idea.
I have no idea.
I was like everybody, a bull, just-- just thinking for food.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Moishe, but I'm talking about the second one, Moishe [NON-ENGLISH].
His father was a big rabbi and very religious, this boy.
When he came.
No.
No.
No.
Nothing.
I'm a little confused with this, if this was straight from the-- from Lager I.
But I'm positive that I was back, they took back.
And then from there, they build up the group of people for the march.
So I'm a little confused because--
No.
Everybody, like you come up in the morning, and you stay in your-- from your block.
You stay in it.
And then we went out.
The-- the-- the-- each time they went out from the camp and they put you on the road.
And you know, and-- and then-- then the German came with the wagons, pushing-- the guys were pushing their food, the ammunition.
And every 50 feet or whatever was soldiers on each side watching with dogs and everything.
And we start marching.
You heard shooting once in a while.
After a few days walking, people couldn't walk.
They give you nothing.
Nothing.
I don't remember ever getting-- get anything there.
Oh, no.
Five, five in a row.
Sure.
Sure.
We go-- ?] they gonna the-- the ways they picked, they picked not main roads.
They picked through farms, little towns.
And people stood in the windows and the doors and looking to us.
I remember, oh my God, so God help me.
I-- I-- we passed by a bakery and the bread smell, oh my God.
This was-- [SIGHS] oh, we take-- and the people were watching.
They stood in the door there, you know, 50 feet away.
And they saw people falling down and-- and there the Germans screaming and shooting.
A few days.
I don't know.
We were sitting down probably somewhere.
No, this what it is-- on the side.
True.
Was no houses, no nothing.
[SIGHS] Their plan was-- was not far already.
Their plan was I think 10 days we've marched.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] They-- they had the plan.
It was a ditch, a natural ditch, mountains around it, mountains.
And was deep, deep.
And this was their plan.
This was mined all the way around to empty out the camp with the people not to leave to them.
Because they saw the Americans and Russians, they're coming closer.
So this was their plan not to give in.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]
A barn.
So they put us up in a barn.
A bigger barn, more people; less barn, less people.
So we were [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] 40 people, 50 people in the barn.
And inside was a wagon what they carry the hay-- you know, those open wagons?
And they stood in there, and we were inside.
No bread, no water, no nothing.
We were there a few hours.
And then a guy came with a-- with a pail and water and a glass.
And he gave us water.
That guy was the farmer.
No, nothing.
Nothing.
And he gave-- he gave us water.
I mean, everybody had a drink of water.
And then we saw there's only one soldier watches us, only one soldier.
He sits by the door, [GERMAN], sits by the door with a big [GERMAN].
He falls asleep.
[NON-ENGLISH].
So he was there a few days.
So I had a few guys with me.
And I said, you know something?
We're going to finish him off.
We're going to go out.
You know, we broke-- I-- on the wagon, I went up on the wagon.
Another two guys went up with me.
And we-- we poke a little at the wood, you know, from the-- these fields, fields with hay, with this, with that-- fields.
[BACKGROUND NOISE] I said, let's go out through here.
We're gonna take up [INAUDIBLE] a boat.
And so it happened.
No.
Didn't run away.
We were two guys and two guys [? were there. ?] If I'm not success, you jump in.
So we took the sticks from this wagon that hold it, took apart.
And we went around this when he's sleeping and the other guy around this.
If I miss, you go, you hit.
And we killed him.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And we ran out.
And in the field was [NON-ENGLISH].
To bring water up there.
But anyway, that's how I remember it.
A well.
And we behind the whole thing in the field, we all 50-- or 45, I don't know-- were laying a whole night in the field, a whole day in the field.
And next day, [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] we saw-- we're laying still, not eating, no-- no we [? chew-- ?] the-- the [INAUDIBLE] [LAUGHS] was going there.
And-- and we saw the farmer looking for us.
So he didn't know, he was stupid.
He didn't know if we went away.
We hide the guy, the-- the policeman, this-- this soldier.
You know, we pulled him with us into the field.
So he probably thought that we all went with him away our barrack.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] [SIGHS]
The Jeep-- the driver sits in the Jeep.
And the guy with the machine gun in the side sits in the back ready with the machine gun.
I didn't know these Americans, I didn't know.
But he goes and he turns.
And they're going slowly because each time they're going in, they're afraid that they're being attacked, you know.
They just-- they went forward, you know.
So a Jeep. 10 minutes later, I see a command car with a big machine in the middle.
And you have four guys already, two with a machine gun, looking, quiet. 10 minutes later, I see a few tanks coming.
Then they're marching the tanks-- and a lot of tanks.
And they stopped.
The whole convoy, you know, stopped.
And soldiers coming out and start smoking, talking.
And we're laying there.
We're afraid.
Maybe they're going
Till he-- he said later on to stand up and with the hand up.
They didn't know who was there.
But when we bend up a little more, you know, he was-- they were shooting in the-- in the air.
It scare us.
So he saw that this is-- this is not Germans with the [NON-ENGLISH].
Because he passed already-- they passed already--
Yeah.
A guy in the back throw a hat to a few guys, you know, the [INAUDIBLE] who had the hat.
You know, he throw away the hat.
So they saw that this is not Germans.
Americans, yeah.
Gave-- everybody gave somebody-- [? black guys ?] were good chauffeurs.
[INAUDIBLE] they were driving the trucks, blacks.
I had one guy that he was Jewish, one guy--
--in the hospital.
He brought another transportation-- another transport with people.
Yes.
Later I--
Yes.
We saw already.
This was Bad Tolz.
Bad Tolz.
I don't know if I'm-- I'm doing right with the name of it, but this was Bad Tolz.
I think.
[LAUGHS] This was Bad Tolz and I knew we were free.
They said, too.
OK.
OK.
OK.
I-- I think you start living.
You come back--
You-- you were-- you were crazy, you know?
You-- you-- you didn't think much because nothing was there.
But your movements and things-- the smile of your face was not a smile but was a cry, but a happy cry.
I'm crying now, a happy cry.
And this what was.
You had-- was no had, was no had.
You were a plain dumb person, plain dumb-- nothing.
You licked from the floor, you-- you-- you eat worms, you eat grass.
I ate grass.
I had a grass that was delicious, sweet as sugar.
Nothing.
We were going like-- like-- like sheeps.
The trucks that took us to the hospital, where the hospital is, [YIDDISH].
I don't know where.
We got-- if this was a crematorium, you go to the crematorium.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
They-- they-- they were clapping on your back, helping you walking into the truck, helping you up to the truck, you know?
They put down things that you can walk up, human.
So-- so it was good.
You saw.
They put you in the hospital.
Oh, no.
You saw that you're going from bad and not for worse.
Right in this town.
A few days.
I was-- I was good.
I was walking, just not eating.
They didn't gave you food.
They gave you just cereal a little bit.
Thousands of people, even from this group, thousands died-- thousands died of dysentery.
Dysentery, how you say it?
Dysentery.
They died.
They couldn't eat, and they came in and ate everything, fill it up.
And they died, destroyed-- thousands, thousands.
I was walking with another guy.
I was walking by foot till Munich.
A nice few miles.
I don't know-- 40, 50.
I don't know.
I was walking to Munich to go to the camps there to see if it's-- because they start making names and a shield on the outside to see who was in the camp, your names.
We start looking--
No.
We were walking.
Yeah.
I didn't have-- I didn't-- oh, how I did connect.
In Germany, I didn't have nobody.
I didn't find anything.
No.
What happened is I was going-- I was in a few camps where they keep the people.
The most was Munich, headquarters.
And they had a list there.
So I-- I-- I-- I knew who was alive and who was dead.
I knew my sister maybe survived, maybe.
And I knew my brother is in Russia.
That's all.
The rest, nothing.
And me.
So what I-- I just looked, actually, for my sister.
Or I look just for Finkelstein.
[SIGHS] When I went to Italy, I saw I have nobody there.
I couldn't stay there, I didn't want to stay there no more.
To Italy, they picked in this camp over there, they picked a group that they can do things.
In Munich, that they can do the-- they are responsible, they are knowledgeable.
They ask you what-- what-- where you come from, who's your father, what did you do.
So that-- so they picked me.
It was Palestine.
But I-- I-- even in Poland, I was a little-- a chalutz already when I joined the organization and everything.
So I had in mind something like that.
I want to live in a commune.
So this was in my mind.
So I have nobody.
I went-- they picked a group of people, about 30 people, 35.
Was-- we had three girls in it, and the rest was boys.
I believe this was people from Israel.
The Bricha, yeah.
For people-- the Bricha [? over ?] the Haganah, they-- they were together but it's two different--
No, not Jewish agency.
This was involved already to do some work.
We didn't know yet nothing.
But we said that the plan is, I want to live in Israel.
So they had me-- so we had-- they picked from a few thousand people this amount.
And we went to Italy.
American trucks.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] American trucks.
And we-- they dropped us off in Santa Maria.
This was the town, a small town on the ocean.
Beautiful.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And they put us in in a house, alone, a private house.
I think if it's right that I remember right that this was a German, a big, rich millionaire.
He was living on this mountain.
And the water's down there, beautiful all the way around with the grapefruit-- not grapefruit--
The-- I'm a [? little out. ?]
No, not little ones.
Not grapefruit.
Yeah, grapefruit.
That's what it is.
Grapefruit, yes.
So around there in a big lake [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] and the ocean on this side.
And what they did is I think they said, you're gonna get food.
You have to cook for yourself, and we're gonna talk to you later.
Don't worry, everything is OK.
You're going to get everything.
Cook and eat and that's it.
And some other people are gonna see you.
So the other people were two people that the-- from the Haganah or whatever.
And they said, you people have a mission.
You have a mission.
So now, this house was not far that you can go into the water.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] So you needed a--
--a boat or something.
And this house was good.
The boat could go underneath the house, you know, it had the bridge over it.
And this owner, the guy who had, was placed space for three cars-- for three boats.
So we had amazing just a few people, not the-- not the whole group.
And we had this meeting and they said what-- what your responsibility is.
The first thing is nothing to talk.
[SPEAKING YIDDISH]
